intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

compositional_truth_check.details: The registry provided (P1-P4) does not match the visual evidence. The observed environment contains only two visible containers: one yellow pot and one black pot. The P3 'White Rabbit' anchor is absent or obscured beyond recognition.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_Yellow_Pot: Present. Contains dark, damp soil. No visible String of Nickels foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Black_Pot: Present. Contains dark, damp soil with white particulate matter (likely perlite or mineral deposits).
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos_White_Rabbit: Systemic Loss. No plant or anchor visible.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty/Bare soil. No signs of active growth.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty/Bare soil. No signs of active growth.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all visible pots. No signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, crystalline or mineral-like debris on the surface of the black pot.
biome_observations.external_objects: A metallic object (spoon) appears in the T-3 image, suggesting human intervention/maintenance.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-4: Minimal change; both pots appear devoid of foliage.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Introduction of a metallic spoon into the yellow pot area.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Removal of the spoon; continued absence of plant life.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Introduction of a white, cylindrical object (possibly a sensor or tube) near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_current: Cylindrical object remains; no biological progression observed.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The pots are devoid of any visible plant matter. The environment appears to be a setup for propagation or soil monitoring rather than an active botanical display. No photosynthesis is occurring.

anomalies: White particulate matter in black pot
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a significant discrepancy between the expected registry and the physical reality. The desk contains two pots, both of which appear to be empty of the specified plant species. The sequence documents the introduction of tools (spoon, sensor) into the soil, suggesting an experimental or maintenance phase rather than a thriving botanical collection. The lack of any green biomass indicates a total systemic loss of the registered specimens.
